user_query:
I'm planning my "Southwest Desert Loop" road trip vlog and need help getting my outreach and planning materials ready. Please use input/roadtrip_plan.json as the sole planning input and produce the following Internet-informed deliverables:

A) Internet research and structured outputs
- For each destination in input/roadtrip_plan.json.destinations, perform search engine queries to identify the official tourism board or city government site and its media/press/communications page.
- Create outputs/research/tourism_contacts.csv with one row per destination and these columns (header required): destination_city, destination_state, official_org_name, official_site_url, media_or_press_page_url, contact_email_or_form, press_kit_or_assets_url, content_guidelines_summary (<=300 chars, in your own words), query_used, date_accessed (YYYY-MM-DD). Only use official sources (e.g., city/state government domains or the city/region’s official tourism organization). If a field isn’t available, use "N/A" but still fill query_used and date_accessed.
- Log what you searched in outputs/research/search_log.jsonl. One JSON object per line with: destination_city, destination_state, queries (array of strings you actually used), chosen_result_title, chosen_result_url, rationale (<=200 chars on why it’s the official source), timestamp (ISO 8601).

B) Draft communications (do not send anything)
- Create a reusable outreach email template at outputs/communications/outreach_email_template.txt. Use 3–5 short paragraphs (< ~220 words total) and include these placeholders: [[TRIP_TITLE]], [[DESTINATION]], [[DATES]], [[ORG_NAME]], [[MEDIA_PAGE_URL]], [[PRESS_KIT_URL]], [[KEY_MESSAGE]]. The template should briefly introduce the trip, mention the sponsor key message and deliverables from the input file, request filming guidelines/permits or media contacts, and propose a quick call.
- Using the first destination in the input file (the first in the destinations array), generate a personalized preview by filling the template with the corresponding data from outputs/research/tourism_contacts.csv and input/roadtrip_plan.json. Save it as outputs/communications/outreach_email_preview_[City]_[State].txt (e.g., outputs/communications/outreach_email_preview_Moab_UT.txt). If any field is unavailable in the CSV, insert "TBD" and append a bracketed note like [Missing: PRESS_KIT_URL]. Ensure values used in the preview match the CSV row for that destination exactly (aside from TBD markers).

C) Meeting notes and action items
- Prepare a planning call notes document at outputs/meetings/planning_call_notes.md that I can share with my collaborators. Include:
  • Title with the trip name from the input and the current date.
  • Participants: “Me” plus every collaborator listed in the input file.
  • Discussion points: for each destination, list 1–2 filming ideas derived from its "focus" array in the input, and reference one outreach target by official_org_name from the CSV.
  • Decisions: summarize which org(s) to contact per destination and any constraints (e.g., note the drone restrictions from the input constraints section).
  • Action items: at least 5 concrete tasks with owners (names from the input’s collaborators or “Me”), clear due dates that fall on or before the corresponding destination dates, and brief descriptions (e.g., “Alex: verify drone restrictions near Arches NP – Due 2026-05-09”).
  • References section linking to the locally created files relative to the workspace (the CSV, the JSONL search log, and the email template).

Important notes
- Use search engine queries to find the official sources; do not rely on third-party blogs or fan pages. Do not include direct URLs in this request; you will determine and record them in the outputs.
- Each destination in the input must correspond to exactly one row in outputs/research/tourism_contacts.csv and at least one entry in outputs/research/search_log.jsonl. The personalized email preview must correspond to the first destination in the input by order.
- Keep all outputs as plain text, CSV, or JSONL. Do not send any emails or messages—only draft them locally.

Input path:
- input/roadtrip_plan.json

Output paths to create:
- outputs/research/tourism_contacts.csv
- outputs/research/search_log.jsonl
- outputs/communications/outreach_email_template.txt
- outputs/communications/outreach_email_preview_[City]_[State].txt for the first destination
- outputs/meetings/planning_call_notes.md All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
